condition: Requires 345MHz daughtercard installed in IQ Panel 2+

condition: Both use 319.5MHz proprietary RF. 2GIG sensors are compatible with Interlogix panels.

condition: Vivint cameras are proprietary and only work with the Vivint Smart Hub ecosystem.

SiX series sensors work with ProSeries PROA7PLUS natively.
ADT Command panel uses SiX series (branded RF6) as primary sensor protocol.
limitations: ADT platform only — no Alarm.com integration

condition: IQ Pro has native PowerG support (915MHz FHSS). DSC PowerG sensors work directly.

condition: Vista 20P is a hardwired panel. Requires separate 5881ENH or 5881ENM wireless receiver module to support ANY 5800 wireless sensors.
limitations: Requires separate wireless receiver module purchase
Max wireless zones depends on receiver model

condition: SiX series uses encrypted 2.4GHz protocol. Vista panels have no SiX receiver. SiX ONLY works with Lyric, ProSeries PROA7PLUS, and ADT Command.
PowerG is a proprietary DSC/Johnson Controls encrypted protocol (915MHz FHSS). 2GIG panels have no PowerG receiver.
PowerG is exclusive to DSC/Qolsys ecosystem. Honeywell panels cannot receive PowerG transmissions.
First Alert Z-Wave smoke/CO detector is the ONLY non-Ring sensor that can trigger Ring Alarm when armed. Specific exception to Ring's third-party restriction.
limitations: Only smoke/CO detection triggers alarm, not a general security sensor

condition: Ring Alarm pairs third-party Z-Wave sensors but they CANNOT trigger the alarm when armed. They function only as smart home devices, not security devices.
limitations: Will NOT trigger alarm when system is armed
No alarm notification to monitoring center
Functions as smart home device only

condition: Qolsys S-Line sensors operate at 319.5MHz. Honeywell Vista panels use 345MHz receivers only.
2GIG GC2 uses Z-Wave 500-series chip without S2 security support. Z-Wave locks will pair with S0 security only (weaker encryption).
limitations: No S2 security support on GC2
Locks pair with S0 only (less secure)
Some newer locks may refuse to pair without S2

condition: Native Frontpoint sensor on Frontpoint Hub. Full feature support.

condition: SimpliSafe is a CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. SimpliSafe sensors use proprietary 433MHz protocol (OOK) and ONLY work with SimpliSafe base stations. Not compatible with any third-party panel.

SimpliSafe CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. Not compatible with Qolsys or any non-SimpliSafe panel.
SimpliSafe CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. Not compatible with Ring or any non-SimpliSafe panel.
SimpliSafe CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. Despite both using 433MHz, SimpliSafe uses proprietary OOK protocol incompatible with DSC.
SimpliSafe CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. No third-party sensors work with SimpliSafe base stations. Only SimpliSafe Gen 3 sensors are accepted.
ADT Command is based on Resideo ProSeries. PROSiX sensors (2.4GHz) are natively compatible.
limitations: ADT Command can only be monitored by ADT — cannot transfer to third-party monitoring

condition: ADT Command only supports SiX/PROSiX sensors (2.4GHz). No 345MHz receiver. Honeywell 5800-series sensors are not compatible.
ADT Command only supports SiX/PROSiX sensors (2.4GHz). No 345MHz receiver.
ADT Self Setup sensors are PROPRIETARY. Despite using 319.5MHz, they use a proprietary protocol that only works with the ADT Self Setup hub.
ADT Self Setup sensors are PROPRIETARY. Not compatible with Qolsys or any non-ADT-Self-Setup panel.
ADT Self Setup sensors are PROPRIETARY. Not compatible with 2GIG or any non-ADT-Self-Setup panel.
ADT Self Setup hub only accepts its own proprietary sensors. Standard 319.5MHz sensors are not compatible.
Vivint 345MHz sensors are rebranded 2GIG hardware. CAN be factory-reset and re-learned into any 2GIG panel.
limitations: Must be factory-reset/excluded from Vivint hub first

condition: Vivint 345MHz sensors work with any panel that accepts 345MHz. ProSeries PROA7PLUS has built-in 345MHz receiver.
limitations: Must be factory-reset from Vivint hub first

condition: Vivint 345MHz sensors work with Qolsys IQ Panel 4 (built-in 345MHz support).
limitations: Must be factory-reset from Vivint hub first

condition: Ring sensors use proprietary Z-Wave pairing. Despite being Z-Wave devices, they ONLY pair with Ring Alarm base stations.
Ring sensors ONLY pair with Ring Alarm base stations. Proprietary Z-Wave pairing — not compatible with other Z-Wave hubs.
Ring sensors CLOSED ECOSYSTEM. Proprietary Z-Wave pairing. Not compatible with Qolsys.
Ring Alarm uses Z-Wave only. No 345MHz RF receiver. Honeywell 5800-series sensors not supported.
First Alert ZCOMBO-G is the ONLY third-party Z-Wave sensor whitelisted to trigger Ring Alarm when armed.
limitations: Only Z-Wave smoke/CO sensor that can trigger Ring Alarm — other third-party Z-Wave sensors cannot trigger alarm

condition: Ecobee thermostats are WiFi-only (NO Z-Wave). Cannot pair directly with panel. Thermostat control available via Alarm.com cloud-to-cloud API integration only.
limitations: Cloud API integration only — not direct panel control
Requires Alarm.com account with thermostat service plan
No local control if internet is down

condition: Abode sensors use proprietary AbodeRF 433MHz protocol (Climax OEM). ONLY work with Abode hubs. Not compatible with any other panel.
Abode uses proprietary AbodeRF protocol at 433MHz — NOT standard DSC 433MHz. Incompatible despite same frequency.
Abode only accepts AbodeRF 433MHz sensors. Standard 345MHz/319.5MHz sensors not supported. Z-Wave peripherals work but NOT as alarm sensors.

limitations: Requires ELK-M1XRF2H receiver module (sold separately)

condition: Elk 319 Series sensors use 319.5MHz and are cross-compatible with GE/Interlogix panels.
